item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dear Strongbad,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe, lol? It's entirely possible that it might just be coincidence. Some people do weird stuff in the car.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If one were to use all of these gestures in a single drive and be observed by Foundation personnel, would they, then, become a PoI?

… asking for a friend …

ETA: Forgot to add that this is probably one of the best skips I've seen so far in Series V.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could just imagine there being an inverse SCP-4093 that literally does everything opposite of SCP-4093. And then a conversation like this happens:

 SCP-4093 drives up to -SCP-4093 at a stoplight and waves. -SCP-4093 flips off SCP-4093. SCP-4093 and -SCP proceed to roll down their windows. SCP-4093: You doin' ok there, buddy? -SCP-4093: Fuck you! SCP-4093: Alright then, you have a good day. -SCP-4093: Suck my ass! -SCP-4093 Throws a cup full of hot coffee at SCP-4093 while SCP-4093 drives away gives him a thumbs up. Nothing interesting in traffic happens for the rest of the day.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, well.. it's not a typical Keter. It's only that by virtue of it being impossible to contain physically.

Thanks for reading! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to admit, my first reaction was "A Keter? From Zyn? Does not compute!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, for some reason, my initial reaction to "Anti-Road Rage Spell" was "that would be a hilarious cover band name. Maybe for a band that does ukulele covers of heavy metal songs."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something about the idea of an angry driver doing everything in his power to drive as dangerously and ineffectively as possible.

I've seen it happen! Some people are really pressed for time. Those people make me worried. Too bad there isn't a pattern of horn honks that means "Stay strong! You can do it! I believe in you!"

Thank you for the +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 to the anti-road rage spell!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The driver of the vehicle in question had appeared to be extremely irate, and was also attempting to use a mobile phone and eat from a small container at the same time.

I'm not quite sure why, but this made me laugh. Something about the idea of an angry driver doing everything in his power to drive as dangerously and ineffectively as possible.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're not willing to risk getting gunned down at the local Italian place by a thumb-shaped man in a suit and fedora while enjoying your carbonara, do you really deserve to meet other SCP fans?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, my latent hope is for there eventually to be a way SCP fans can recognize each other without speaking (secret SCP handshake anyone?), but there's always the problem of hand gestures potentially being seen as obscene or threatening gang violence or the like…

Drive safe, kids!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Time to test this next time I’m ouside. I dunno, maybe it’ll work.

item.title: Re: Author Post!
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It took me awhile to think of it. A lot of the inspiration came from the idea of hand signals used by drivers/bikers, and the mystical hand motions used by stage magicians and in fantasy-genre magic.

item.title: Re: Author Post!
item.content:encoded:
okay hand:the SCP
I really like the idea that your SCP tries to explore,i have never seen a phenomenon SCP this unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thirded

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't it be really hard to type with crab hands?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How can we be sure you're not a butterfly-crustacean hybrid? 🤔

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean… usually I just let pedestrians go first because I feel less anxious that way. :P Lot of doggos in the area near home and they get walked at all hours of the day.

Thank you for the +1 for road safety

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, thanks for the nice comment! I'm definitely a fan of the short and simple Series I premises; it's just a matter of trying to find approaches that haven't been done before…

Thank you for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that it's short. I love that it's oddly specific. I love that you eschewed the interview that would almost always show up in these kinds of skips.

Thanks! :) You did help out a lot in the editing stages with trimming down the wordiness.

I love that the Foundation both understands it, and is completely in the dark.

It was something new-ish I was trying out, so I'm glad you like it!

Thanks again for the upvote and for reading this in chat. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did try to make the effects seem like things that could be recorded empirically (the D-Class who ran the test trials were probably very, very confused) and were things that someone who had no clue about the anomaly might try measuring.

made me think of all the Foundation researchers who are just professionals coming in and doing their jobs.

Haha, indeed! Real-life science can be pretty bland and tedious at times, so I like to think that part of the draw of working for the Foundation is getting assigned to weird, but harmless projects.

Thanks for the upvote! Congrats on your first vote on the site!! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's okay. I am fine stating straightforwardly that I do not actually have crab hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is just Zyn telling us what she does when she’s at an intersection with a pedestrian. No self-inserts Zyn! :P

(+1 for road safety)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a second I was unsure about the Keter class, but in the end it all seemed to work out pretty nicely. Short and simple, this SCP reminds me of a lot of series I skips, and I love it! Thanks for the great work Zyn +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zyn gets a pass for being Zyn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No role-playing in the forums

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love that it's short. I love that it's oddly specific. I love that you eschewed the interview that would almost always show up in these kinds of skips. I love that the Foundation both understands it, and is completely in the dark.

Cute anomaly, with plenty left to the imagination. Good work as usual Zyn, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if it was intentional, but I really like how this SCP illustrates Foundation procedure and motivation.
The report is comprehensive and detailed despite the anomaly being so innocuous and abstract. Most SCPs are either highly dangerous or just plain bizarre and so it's easy to imagine the in-universe attention they receive as necessity or curiosity. This is the first one I've read so far (I'm only a few hundred skips into the mainlist) that's made me think of all the Foundation researchers who are just professionals coming in and doing their jobs.

+1 from me, and my first vote ever! Woo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(\/)(;,,;)(\/)

-gives you da pasta- (with crab hands)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GIVA ME DA PASTA +1

item.title: Author Post!
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so… this was in progress for over a month. I never really was able to write more than a few sentences each time I tried to sit down and work on this. I was originally inspired by a cool person I encountered while driving to work.

Big, big thanks to the chat reviewers who read this over!

  • Weryllium for phrasing fixes, word choice, and commenting on the specific good points (ETA: also, providing the name!)
  • fieldstone for commenting on the containment and length
  • Lixerf for talking about hand signals
  • Everyone else who chimed in about GIVA DA PASTA PLS and crab hands

This was an exercise in presenting an essentially completely harmless Keter anomaly that the Foundation thinks they understand… but even with all the data they’ve collected, they really don’t know what they’re up against.

/crab-hand-signal-walks away
